reviews.review_text:
This wasn't made with an intention to drive the player desperate, sad or hopeless. This was made by someone who loves humanity and respects your time. Genuinely one of the greatest examples of media where the journey is as good as the destination.
If you love horror media and can't help but empathise with weird monster freaks, this game is for you. Just play it, stop reading the reviews.

One of the best RPGs I've ever played. Honestly this one took me by surprise, I was looking for something like Fear and Hunger and this was exactly what I was looking for and more. Please support this creator, I'll die unfulfilled if this game never gets a sequel

Look Outside is a beautifully deranged masterpiece. It lures you in with atmosphere and curiosity, then punishes your confidence with creatures that look like they were designed by someone who hasn’t slept in three days and has seen too much. Every time I thought, “I can handle one more room,” the game politely reminded me that I absolutely could not.

The progression system is brutal but fair, you die, you learn, you adapt. The multiple endings are genuinely worth hunting down, each one adding another layer to the game’s strange, unsettling world. The soundtrack is so good I’ve added several tracks to my driving playlist, which has made my commute feel like an eldritch pilgrimage.

The art direction is insane in the best way. Whoever designed these environments deserves both absolute praise and a wellness check. I want to lick their brain.

There was a point I was faced with a decision and I thought I was just following the brutal rules of the world. When I went back to the apartment and looked myself in the mirror he thought "Was there another way...?" I restarted the game so quick and found another way because the game held up a mirror and made me feel bad for my decisions.

For the price, this game is an absolute steal.

Gaze upon the visitor for he is...GLORIOUS....skdhfskjfdh jksh!!!!!!

Pros:
1. Good story.
2. Good gameplay.

Cons:
1. Lack of some quality of life features. (For example, you can't just quit game when you don't want to play anymore, you need to do another 20 minuter run just to be able to finally save and quit.)

Look Outside is a great game! Horror-wise, I appreciate the fact that it doesn't have many jumpscares, but it's more cosmic-eldritch-body horror, with unsettling but really cool pixel art sprites and descriptions of scenes that make me wonder what the hell I just read for the next few days.

It's also just a really fun game. It's a pretty difficult RPG where you have to manage resources like your health, healables, and weapons to survive. You have limited opportunities to save the game also, only getting to after you sleep or after you explore a new area (but if you don't like that, there is a game mode where there are auto saves + free manual saves). Combat is great, party members have stamina that can be used for more powerful skills, but different party members have their own unique skills to them that give them each their own style of fighting. You usually have to go out of your way to figure out how to recruit party members, as many have you solve a sort of puzzle to get them.

There's a lot to do in this game. It pretty regularly gets updates and as of now there's like 13 different endings. Lore wise, there's always more going on than you think. Oh, you've gotten 4 different endings and gotten more than half the achievements and you think you've figured out the story? Here's two more endings and an entire new world for you to explore related to this character you thought had little to no importance. I've gone down so many rabbit holes. So many. This game goes deep...

With all that, Look Outside is a very long and pretty difficult game, and there are a lot of things you can miss if you don't know how things work. I guess a big part of the game is figuring out the world and coming back in another playthrough and seeing what you can do. My advice: when someone wants something, give it to them or do it, and make sure to note any highlighted text...
Please make this 100% steam deck compatible Id love to play this on the fly! Outside of that I dont get much time to play, but I love the idea of this game and how it plays! Thanks devs.

This is an easy fill-in for a spot in the top 5 RPGs of all time and flat-out the best RPG maker game out there.  

It took me 30+ hours to beat the game once. It was an amazing experience. Come to find out I've only seen 1/10th of what the game has to offer content-wise. 

One dude made this game. For 10 dollars you cannot find anything better. It's a masterpiece. Even if you're not big into horror (like me), you'll find a LOT to love. 

If you're still on the fence about buying, PLEASE hop off it and go play this ♥♥♥♥ NOW.

Look Outside is a survival-horror RPG about being stuck in your apartment building during the end of the world. How exactly is the world ending? Good question! For reasons which are gradually revealed over the course of the game, anyone who is visually exposed to the outside world is turned into a monster. Based on exposure some people become slightly monsterish, perhaps growing some extra limbs, or extremely monsterish, perhaps growing multiple additional purple tentacled bodies and expanding out through several rooms. The forms they take are often determined by the obsessions they held before exposure, so a teething infant might be, say, obsessed with teeth… Anyway. You meet your neighbors, most of whom have become something somewhat different than human. And, in many cases, they’re still chill, still basically good people who suddenly have a Big Problem. I found Look Outside an incredible experience because it contained such contrast between disturbing and absurd, human and alien. Trying to tackle this apocalyptic problem entirely within this apartment building full of weirdos creates one of the most human experiences I’ve had playing a single-player game – we don’t know who we can trust or what they’re hiding, but in the end all we’ve got is each other.

The combat, development quality, creepy art style, and the overall mechanics warm my jaded tired heart. This game is so beautifully freaky and unique that I strongly recommend anyone even remotely curious and up for a challenge to try it out.
Excellent sprite work, interesting cast of characters and a legitimately well written story. A must for all RPG Maker horror fans.

Look Outside is a delightful game, which may sound odd with the horrific setting and subject matter. The writing shines with a great emphasis on empathy and can be really touching as you and your friends ride out the apocalypse. The combat system is serviceable, even satisfying at time, but the exploration and puzzles are great. 

All in all one of my favorite game in recent memory. Ratthew, my beloved.
This game is so good i straight up didnt look outside for a full real life day

Incredible style, incredible story, very awesome
One of my fav games honestly

It's a special one, for sure. The Atmosphere, the characters, the vibes. All on point. If you look at the screenshots or trailer and feel like this might be your thing then it most definitely is and you will NOT be disappointed.
I would highly recommend this game. The developer is great  and is actively updating the game. The game can be challenging and the 100% is fun (although it took a lot of time).

Look Outside mixes survival and RPG elements to create an excellent, fun, and unique experience. People in your apartment building are transforming into grotesque creatures and you have up to 15 days to resolve this somehow. The themes center on body horror and cosmic horror and the character designs and art are masterfully done. Music is also very strong and many of the boss fights have unique music -- it has one of my favorite video game OSTs. The writing quality is also commendable.

There is a ton of content, including dozens of boss fights and many colorful party members to recruit (and with their own sidequests). Almost all of the dungeons are optional, so you can just pick and choose blindly for your own personalized experience and get multiple fresh playthroughs. I played on Easy, which lets you save anytime you want (versus at the beginning of each day). This makes the game more like a traditional turn-based RPG with less survival aspects, but this was still enjoyable, especially since it made experiencing alternative branches / dungeons more approachable.

Highly recommended, and a must-play for RPG/horror fans.
Easily one of my all time greatest games. It bucks so many trends and have so much content I havent even scratched at.

the soundtrack alone makes this game an A-tier at the minimum.
backtracking sucks tho

I was watching one of those game over compilation videos for Point and Click games and there was one for Look Outside. I got interested after discovering that the player had some of the mutated characters in his party. I always get interested in playing an apocalyptic game where you can play as or befriend someone clearly infected but able to stay sane.

This is definitely one of the best RPGMaker games I played as of late.
The character writing is really good, you get a feel for how everyone is personality-wise. Depending on what party members you have, they'll even occasionally interact with what's going on or with each other.

Aside from the engaging writing, I also love the mechanics that make the gameplay not boring.
Namely, I love the save system. After leaving your apartment, you build a danger meter as you explore new areas. The first tier allows you to save your progress upon returning home, and tiers after that give a higher XP bonus.

All in all, I would like to do a lot more to Lyle than kiss him twice.
i love the turn based combat, the variety of weapons and playable characters to unlock, and the enemies you have to fight in the game :3
Not exactly the type of game I was expecting when I bought this (not a fan of turn-based combat) but I like the idea and the humor.

To be honest, i started with fear and hunger. That game for sure isn't for everyone this game tho is just what i've been searching for. A perfect balance of fear, funky music and thrilling combat. Best indie game i played probably. HIGHLY recommend giving this game a try.

Goddang fantastic game, anything that needs to be said has already been said by the other reviews.
This game really puts most other games to shame, especially rpg maker ones since this one works perfectly and buttery smooth.
A very fun and charming experience that's absolutely packed with wonderful content. Well worth the price if you like survival horror or JRPGs.
full of love and heart and creativity and passion

I watched Vargskelethor's gameplay, and I just HAD to buy this game, despite probably not even truly playing it myself. It's just this good, and it has so much respect for its players. It's truly a gem. You should try it, whatever considerations or doubts you might have... Just buy it blindly. It'll surprise you a lot.
I carefully considered the weight of my words before saying it, but, I think I mean it when I say this has to be the #2 jrpg-style game of this decade after Deltarune.

Despite it all ! Because again, you might have many doubts, considering it's a pretty simple-looking rpg-maker game, but it does it so well it goes into that "Classics" category instantly. It's gore, it's mesmerizing, it's cute sometimes, funny, humane as much as it is twisted. This is an experience you need to try.

The developer just needs every bit of support and love we can possibly give them because they deserve at least that.

Fun game in a very interesting world and environment. Any fans of RPG and horror I would recommend. Quality pixel graphic game. So many choices and possible paths to take, plenty of replay value here.

I bought and beat this game on release and came back to it for the 2.3 update. So much effort has been put into expanding and improving this game, it's honestly amazing. On release I felt like it was undercooked, didn't bother to get the true ending or perfect ritual. Now I got the final true ending and a few other endings on cursed mode. Cursed mode is difficult, but I highly recommend it. I had such a great time on my cursed mode playthrough, despite my best effort there is still plenty of content I never had the chance to see. I wish more games got this post release treatment, it feels like a completely different game from release. For this much content at $10 it's an extremely good deal. If you enjoy fear & hunger, great art, interesting stories, and challenging fights, Look Outside is a great time.

Dude. What a sleeper hit of 2025 that I just got into. I started it on recommendation from my brother, and could not put it down. So many secrets, so many neat story bits, and SO much stuff to do. 56 hours in, and I am STILL finding out new interactions, solutions, secrets, and just stuff to do. I have areas I have not fully explored still, I have characters I have not used yet. The soundtrack is phenomenal. Awesome of the dev to include a free game with the soundtrack. Visuals are great. Off putting in the best way horror can be. But what makes this really special I think, are the characters and story being told. It starts kind of generic, but turns to something super unique. Without spoiling anything, this is a game BEGGING to be replayed, for the player to try new things, and learn it's systems inside and out. If you want to play blind (optimal experience), expect to reset a few times. And try new things. Like Fear and Hunger, this is kind of a sandbox to learn about. Alternatively, using the wiki very briefly can help alleviate some resets. Awesome game. I can't wait to dive back in again.
goated jrpg. first one ive played that has party variance which makes this more replayable than other games like this

Brilliant little RPG Maker game that, like most of its kind, does a LOT with a little.

You're Sam, an apparent loner living in an apartment building during the end times. Some cosmic horror has arrived on Earth, and anyone who looks at it turns into an insane abomination, thus you're not supposed to look outside. Said cosmic horror will go away in 15 in-game days, so it's up to you to survive and reach an ending on that timeslot.

A good summary for the experience is "light Fear and Hunger". Like F&H, this is a simple JRPG that doubles as a survival sim where the fun is reacting to the world around you, choosing what to do next and finding out its many secrets and surprises at your own pace as you look for recruitable characters to build a party to slowly make yourself more powerful and fit for dealing with the at first overpowered horros that plague the building.
Unlike F&H, this game is much more scrutable and user-friendly, not built to piss you off with its brutal difficulty, lack of tutorials or general relevant information relayed to the player, and one-off death traps. Not to say Fear and Hunger is bad, but it's doing something else, and Look Outside offers another, easier version of that I find much more interesting and satisfying.

The general vibe ping pongs between "deadly serious and horrifying" to "funny, light-hearted and self-aware", it hits that sweet Earthbound-like spot. There's so much character and wonderful and evocative elements to its story that I couldn't help but get immersed in the whole thing, the routine of every game day, slowly peeling away at new sections of the building or becoming capable of clearing areas previously too hard was incredibly fun.
There's the ocasional frustrating moments when you choose a dialogue that leads to game over, when RNG doesn't play in your favor for things like status effects or weapons breaking, some sidequests feeling half-baked or realizing time has passed too quickly and wasn't well-spent, losing too much progress before you could save, etc. But these are very small grievances compared to all this game does well.

If you enjoyed F&H, you'll probably like this one while thinking it's too easy. If you bounced from F&H due ot its brutal difficulty, you'll find a more pallatable version of that here.

Its such a big game its worth every penny, almost 20hrs and i feel like i barely gone deep enough through the soil. the mood set up is so good i was so tense for the first half of the game. even after i get used to the game things still get me on the edge of my seat. on the other side there are touching moments to this game that i just love so much, like the people you help... and the people who help... and the helpless... 

very good game :-) oh i love the art style too, so inspirating.
Very good game, try it, The RPG Mechanic and story make u wanna finish the game, and the uniqueness of how to obtain something
Absolutely fantasic visuals and music, with highly effective horror and atmosphere. A must-play in both the RPG and Horror category.

Found out about this game through dosh doshington, you didn't give me a discount tho :(
glad you two are friends, great so far looking forward to raging at losing all my progress
Incredible game the dev is awesome it reminds me of fear and hunger. The pixel art and setting are really neat.
Tight, memorable game with old school inspiration. Best enjoyed spoiler-free. Once I was blind, but now... I SEE.

I love old-school RPGs.  I especially love when there's a large cast of party members and when combat can vary greatly.  Mixed strategies are fun!  Look Outside delivers well in this regard.  Different party compositions and combat strategies keep things interesting.  Additionally, I've always had a soft spot for eldritch horror themes, and Look Outside is filled to the writhing, tentacled BRIM with it.  

If you want fun turn-based combat and a dark, mysterious world to explore, this is a must-play.
Incredible game. Art, music, characters, all winners. Easy recommend.

This game is incredibly detailed and rich with its story and characters- I played it over and over again just to get different party members and see their interactions with one another and the things in the apartment building. The gameplay is fun and the difficulty settings make it easy for anybody to enjoy. I spent most of my time playing 'Explorer Mode' (Easy) which is more forgiving and has an autosave feature, but I know a lot of people love the more challenging difficulties. I don't really enjoy horror as a genre but I found this game very palatable. I've been recommending it to everybody because of how much I love these characters and their stories. It's really well made and the writing and visuals are both incredible.

Honestly I've always hated RPGs, and I don't know why. Until I played this game.

Look Outside is honestly my favorite game of all time. The music is amazing, the fights are really cool, the art is absolutely ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ gorgeous, and the story is really ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ good. The usage of body horror is actually really cool, especially with a few of the endings (WHICH I WONT SPOIL, GO INTO THIS GAME BLIND IF YOU CAN!!!!). Also, the mechanic of having companions potentially be cursed is also really cool. Overall, buy this game if you can. For the love of The Visitor- i mean uhh God, get it.
pretty good game the only complaint i have is the stupid enemy in the ground floor it keeps on killing me on the first turn and it seems completely random so that i lose all of my progress
absolute MASTERCLASS of a Horror RPG. ABSOLUTELY RECOMMEND to any players who enjoy games like Buckshot Roulette or any horror RPG games in the genre.

literally one of my favourite games of all time. the story is beautiful. all of the endings are interesting to think about and theyre all so different. a million little secrets. i feel like i could play it forever. the art and music are so tasty and gorgeous. i dont even know how to express how much i love it and you should get it.
i was so immersed with the game i refused to look outside for 15 days!! still ended up with mutations...
This game makes my skin crawl with the body horror elements and feels very unique, if you like horror and rpg then definetly check this out
i personally love the unique monsters, the story, and all the conent in general its a great and unique experience

While I feel that "Look Outside" is a title that is quite steep in its learning curve and takes quite awhile to get a grasp on mechanics and intended methods of play, I also have to say that the story, artstyle, gameplay, monster design, and character design are all top notch. I feel the largest issue with this game is how difficult it can be for new players. Overall best RPG maker style game I have ever spent time on.

A wonderful piece of art. I would highly recommend not looking anything up your first playthrough. It leaves you on edge for every decision and interaction. You will probably need to look stuff up to see a lot of the endings though, after your blind playthrough.

This is generally not my type of game, but I have always loved cosmic horror so I figured I would give it a shot.

I was very happy I did. The atmosphere, music, art, story, it is all incredible. The game does require a lot of deduction and can feel very overwhelming at times, often the seemingly right decisions are the wrong ones. Your choices are questioned when brushing your teeth "Did I really have to go into that home and kill those monsters, sure they attacked me but I essentially broke into their home. They were just living." it makes you question every decision. Seemingly friendly things can either provide massive help or a game over screen. You never know.

The story itself hooks you, and they drip feed you information enough to keep you engaged but not spoil it. The conclusion is well worth the wait and does not disappoint. There are multiple endings. Some good, most bad. But the overall situation is a dreadful existential crisis showing our insignificance in a way that frankly left me in awe. 

Overall well worth the money. I wish there was more information on how things like social, hygiene, and stress function. It is not really explained at all and needs to be looked up to understand. Some minor bugs such as the targeting glitch can also be frustrating.

Incredible game! I normally do not enjoy media in the horror genre, but gave this game a shot because I enjoy RPG/JRPG type games and it had a lot of positive reviews. Very glad I did! I've now completed three runs through the game and still want to do more... 

Look Outside has everything I love in a good game:
-Great story and characters
-Fun and challenging gameplay
-Fantastic music throughout

This game is a 10/10
if you like fear and hunger, play this game, if you dont, play it anyway or i will blend your cat
I love this games artistic design and level design. I truly love how there are many outcomes to many characters.

I absolutely loved the game. I get stuck at some points and have to take a break, but overall the atmosphere is incredibly tense, even for a game of this digital caliber, and the monsters and psychological conundrums brought up by the game by the, incredibly well-thought out and developed, characters, make playing through the game rewarding and interesting. The monster design is also absolutely phenomenal.

Amazing game, you start playing without expecting much, but as you realize this isn't your typical outdated turn based game, it just keeps getting more and more interesting.
Weapon durability seems to be a nuisance at first, but it's actually very manageable and understandable, as some of the arsenal is extremely busted. Each character has unique skills with different playstyles, and there are so many of them that you will 100% not be able to test everyone in one run.
Theres a limit of 15 days before the game automatically ends, and each day takes about two to three hours, so the pacing feels pretty good. There are some mechanics that reminds me of No, I'm Not a Human, Persona and the old Final Fantasy games, if you are interested in that.
One of my favorite genres is Lovecraftian Horror, and oh god, they nailed it here, but this is best left for you to discover yourself :)

Look outside -- хорошая игра-представитель JRPG-хоррора с высокой реиграбельностью. 

Разработчики смогли удачно совместить жанр сюрвайвал хоррор (Сайлент Хилл) с мощным арт-направлением (в игре яркие тона). Классная атмосферная музыка и сочные красочные арты прекрасно дополняют эту видеоигру тем, что создают оригинальную атмосферу нердовской культуры, отсылок и... сильной паники (в виду общей "сплаттерпанковости").
 
Сюжет? Срез жизни типичного дотера: 1) Не моется. 2) Не чистит зубы. 3) Безработный. Но, в итоге, [spoiler] Иван-дурак (Сэм) всё делает по-красоте и побеждает... нечто. [/spoiler] Сюжет мне очень понравился, пусть я и являюсь придирчивым.

Посмотри Наружу!
Good game. Remember to double check who's at the apartment door

Pros:
- The game's art direction is decent, most of the areas feel haunting and the monsters look grotesque
- There’s a substantial amount of content and achievements for those who love to have high replayability and their money well-spent
- Accessibility is great for an indie RPGMaker game

Cons:
- The relationships between the characters feel disconnected and poorly developed
- The music often feels out of place compared to the game’s atmosphere, like when I'm fighting a horrifying looking boss and the music being played felt really upbeat? (not talking about the final boss, but most creepy looking bosses)
- Some dialogues took a really long time to finish but can't be skipped, which is kinda annoying

All things considered, this is the dev's first game and he can't avoid all mistakes. Hence, I'd still recommend this game for those who love RPGMaker

Look Outside is an RPG where you try to survive or live through an apocalyptic event where humans turn to monsters.

I was surprised of how good it is. Usually, I do not enjoy eldritch horror story/game, and I find them to be nonsensical. And there is no emotional story told in this game. But Look Outside is not just a game about eldritch horrors. There is humor and silliness to it. Really enjoyed it for what it is.

I finished my first playthrough of this recently and loved it. The atmosphere is brilliantly creepy, especially early game. There are so many quirky characters you can meet, and the pixel art and character design are endlessly imaginative. And it's accompanied by a great synth-driven John Carpenter-esque soundtrack. 

The game also has a surprising amount of warmth, and there some genuinely heartwarming moments sprinkled in amongst the horror. There are so many choices you can make that actually matter, and branching story paths that lead to different endings. It's definitely worth playing through more than once. This is one of my favourite games I've played so far this year - highly recommended.

Dude, I dont leave reviews okay? Literally never feel the urge to really and thats nothing on the creators and such. But dude, this? I am in complete Shock. You wanna talk about perfectly nailing that lovecraftian cosmic horror feel? bro, you wanna talk about really pulling you in with decisions and genuinely FEELING a type of way while playing? I have never been so compelled by a game before let alone experienced something like this before in my entire life. At 30 years of age I can officially say nothing and I mean NOTHING is going to top this game for me for a long, long time. I know it shows 12 hours currently, but I am telling you within the first hour you are pulled all the way into this. Like I need this game on a global scale lol I want to travel everywhere and yet somehow the creator of this beauty managed to fit it all into a apartment building FILLED with life and stories. I am dumbfounded, in shock, and so going to replay this a ungodly amount of times. At home I am a local artist and you best believe I am going to start sending in work to the creator of this game lol I am beyond impressed with EVERYTHING this game has to offer and show. I have laughed, cried, screamed, felt everything and more....and its a 2D retro style looking game.... like bro, you went above cooking on this one. The comedy, madness, and emotional depth is just.... I am telling you right now, if H.P Lovecraft was around today, he would have been obsessed with this game. I have found zero cons to this game, all freaking pros on my end. Again I never write reviews but this masterpiece deserves insane amounts of attention. I am genuinely willing to give these people all of my twisted artwork just to encourage a continuation of this in a different area or something, its that good to me. We need a new category of Overwhelmingly Positive, seriously it needs to be higher than that. I want EVERYONE TO LOOK OUTSIDE!!!!!!!

Amazing, incredible, stunning, spectacular.
An almost overwhelmingly large foray into madness, set against the mundane backdrop of a single apartment building. Amass your party of freaks, mutants, monsters and weirdos and do battle with beautifully drawn abominations. Rare example of 'Good Cosmic Horror'. 
This game surprised me at several turns with its humanity, it is very cool to me how it challenges you to see recognizable humanity in monstrosities. Even those that dont talk to you can tell a story by their design and context.

Combat is fine. I found myself skipping lesser enemies towards to the end as I was only interested in the bosses and the story but I think that combat is sufficiently deep to tickle the fancies of most tactical RPG enjoyers. Personally I didn't care for it and just let the game optimize my equipment as most enemies are weak to slash damage anyway. I also found that there was so much to do that I didn't get to unlock very many skills for Sam.

I LOVE the endings. There's a couple of joke ones and at least two which are borderline impossible to get without help but the ones I saw were very cool.

Very very good game do recommend!

A fun ride where no one run is ever the same. there's so many optional things to do aside from the areas relevant to the "main objective". all the party members have their own unique gimmicks that set them apart from eachother that you can't really rank imo. they're all equally useful (atleast the one's I found were). the game looks good. I was originally put off by "pixelated rpg maker game #897" but the game looks good and does get creative with the level design and art style. a fun game worth buying

'Look Outside' is a semi-linear RPG with survival horror mechanics wrapped in a cosmic-horror themed adventure with killer pixel art, wicked monsters, awesome music, and endearing characters. Another aspect I appreciate is the high replay-value due to the great amount of content, multiple endings, and unique variable elements that keep this RPG suspenseful and exciting no matter how many times you play it. Absolutely recommend!

This game has good challenge, good art, good music, amazing themes, doesn't take itself to seriously, and it has, by far, the best piece of cosmic horror media I have ever seen. You can tell the creator cares about the game. It shines, it is so cool. 100% my choice for game of the year 1000% recommend. God bless ya'll thanks for reading, I hope ya'll have fun playing if you choose to buy this game.

I had stumbled across this game due to my Youtube algorithm blessing me with a video about one of the endings, and was surprised I hadn’t heard of it prior to this. Some 30 hours later, I find myself placing Look Outside among the top 5 indie games I’ve ever played, enough to compel me to get a review out there in the hopes that I can get more people to experience this creators work.

Look Outside is an old school style RPG, akin to Earthbound and other classics of the SNES era, at least in it’s appearance, but under the hood you rapidly find very interesting systems and complex characters who have a surprising level of depth to them. 
You find yourself dropped into the role of an unemployed shut in, forced to try to survive a 15 day nightmare scenario as the world ends around him, while he is trapped within an apartment complex full of quirky characters and nightmarish creatures straight out of list of fears and phobias. The location and situation going on brought me back to my time enjoying the Sweet Home web comic years ago, keeping me on my toes and locked in till I finally got to the credits rolling.
What you put in, is generally what you will get back out from this title, with you being fully able to spend the duration never engaging with anything and playing video games till time runs out, or digging deep into a shockingly well written story as to why we find ourselves in this end times, and how we might be able to stop it and salvage what is left of the world. Having worked to get what I consider the best ending, I cannot stress enough how interesting this plot was when you get into the murky waters of the truth, and I as a creative myself, I feel better for having experienced this tale in it’s totality.

Combat is similar to the old Dragon Quest games, or Earthbound, with horrific creatures on full display and your party members not actually visible during fights. Most characters are able to have a mix of melee and ranged weaponry, with durability and ammo reserves to consider as you are progressing through the different sections of the apartment building. The main character has a system to learn a variety of abilities, and every other party member has their own unique skills and play styles that gives you a lot of freedom to build your main team around your preferences. And with party members coming from plot and combat choices, and even random encounters via knocks at your apartment door, you may wind up with a completely different roster of friends on different play throughs ( For instance, in my main run I actually never met any of the door knock encounters that could have joined me, RNG be damned. )

Enemies are visible on the over-world map, so encounters are not random, and you have some ability in most cases to dodge them entirely or try to flee if you get stuck engaging, though overcoming a foe does leave it dead for the rest of the game, with a corpse visibly present for the duration of your run in most cases. While this does take away the ability to farm encounters, it fits in this survival setting, as I began to feel like I was securing areas as I progressed, making each day feel like I was taking back a little more of my safety and the building itself. This game does have three difficulty settings that control your ability to save anytime, the prices of items, and even the appearance of completely unique enemies in Cursed Mode. Playing on Explorer ( Easy mode ) likely gave me a lot more freedom to become a monster hunter, but that style likely would not have served me well on higher difficulties, were resource and time management will be more crucial.

The music is themed perfectly for the retro style being presented here, with music tracks shifting the tone of the environments quite well, but the sound effects shine even more than the background tracks. Given the horror setting, this game does very well with jump scare sounds, spooky groans, and all manner of disturbing squelches and growls to fit different things happening on screen. 

If you are at all a fan of RPGs and horror, this is a gem that deserved a lot more attention, and I honestly regret it took me this long to stumble upon it. Hats off to the creator for putting together such an interesting story and characters. Look Outside is a unique title that I think any fan of indies would benefit from experiencing, and I look forward to whatever this creator puts out next (after all, there are some plot points lingering in my brain that I simply NEED more answers to! )

I'm historically not a fan of turn-based RPGs, but the worldbuilding, environments and most notably, the art direction of this game have really gripped me, I've been playing it for days on end. It really scratches a similar itch to Earthbound and Mother 3, though they aren't cited as inspirations. Overall, this game goes above and beyond what is expected from an RPG Maker game.
Important note: Don't play on Survivor difficulty for your first. The limited saves create a lot of situations where you end up losing progress and have to backtrack and re-play areas again. The only way to circumvent this, short of taking lots of risks, is to pass time in the apartment to be able to sleep, and then save afterward. But before you know it, the 15 days will have passed, and you'll be forced into the bad ending.
There's so much creativity, music, and art assets packed inside, simply put, it's a masterpiece.
most interesting and mind breaking game i’ve ever played, ost is crazy, many different endings, generational game ❤️
Buena música, grotesco y te dan ganas de probar todas las posibilidades.

Great soundtrack, tons of bosses, a surprising amount to explore despite being confined to a single building, and lots of replayability.

It's a really good game; worth every cent.

This is such an amazing game I can't recommend it enough. The less you know going the more enjoyment you can get out of it. A horror RPG using 16-bit inspired graphics. Resource management is key and there are a multitude of possibilities An ideal game to book club with friends and discuss your various approaches to things. Straight fire. Holy moly this game has the vibes, the story, and honestly the perfect difficulty for survival horror. Nice mix of, "dear God I can't save I'm freaking out" with, "meh, not the end of the world on this runback".

I don't know why I've been so obsessed with this game. I bought this game on a whim and never regretted it. I've played it so much and loved every minute of it, well with the exception of a few bosses.

like fear and hunger and undertale and to be honest this game is quite addicting to play

This is the kind of game where staying inside pays off.

This is the kind of game where going outside pays off.

This is the kind of game where there's enough seriousness in the stew for genuine dramatic effect and psychological engagement, while at the same time comedy is there for those who want it.

This is the kind of game where a few days in you already feel nostalgic when the main music loop hits.

This is the kind of game where you can experience guilt when you murder someone and satisfaction when you save a life.

This is the kind of game where the normal difficulty will make you consider quitting, and even though you haven't realized it yet, that's a good thing.

This is the kind of game that will stay in your memory for some time. We all know that eventually everything will disintegrate, that your hair will whiten and your teeth will fall off... Indeed, perhaps with time your very flesh will take on new and unsightly forms as you age, but for some years this little gem will leave a modest impact in some region of your brain. It's not a memory that will follow you to the retirement home, but it's good enough.

This is the kind of game you'll buy if you've read this review.

the amount of content in this game is astronomical 83 hours and im still missing like 50 achievments despite beating the game multiple times. The game is a Survival horror +  turn based rpg with very good turn based combat. Each party member feels super unique and plays differently for example one party member increases his health everytime he finishes an enemy in combat alot of his skills use his health instead of his mp and some of his skills damage scales with his health, he is also unable to guard making him an extremly powerful but risky companion if you build him correctly, another companion is a tank with potential for huge dps but heals and mp restoring items are less effective on her with the right build and resources shes a monster but she can also be a huge liability. Theres a total of 18 companions and the fact each one plays differently is geniunley insane many rpgs ive played have a smaller roster and lack the diversity of skills that this game does. Even your main character can be built drastacially differently each run with skills for him being aquired through video games which you have to play in ur apartment and use up valuable in game time. Theres a total of 19 skills to be learned and it's impossible to learn them all in one playthrough, playing these video games brings up dialogue narrating what you are playing but if you have certain party members they also comment on your gameplay and can even join in on the game adding more often funny dialogue. Adding onto skills, weapons and some armor also add additional skills to the characters that equip them adding even more diversity to your party. In normal mode you dont need to worry to much about the super techical stuff you can just throw a party together of characters you like and have a blast however cursed mode is where in my opinion this game truly shines and if youre into more hardcore/ difficult rpgs you will love cursed mode except even if you are dont do it for a first playthorugh cursed mode requires game knowledge you will have a miserable time without this game knowledge. But anyways back to cursed mode it drives the difficulty to the max you need to be spending your resources in a smart way as well as making a good party comps that compliments eachother. Talking themes of the game this game does cosmic horror so well probably one of the best games to ever do it while there is alot of humor and feel good moments in the game theres equally as many horrific and disturbing moments. The art in this game is also great as of writing this there are still some placeholder sprites as a year later this game is still getting updated which is crazy considering the dev is also making another game called malison the cursed city which is another rpg thats supposed to be even bigger than look outside which is insane.

tldr: game is a must for turn based rpg fans

Horrifying, depressing, hopeful, silly. Chillaxing while the world has gone to ♥♥♥♥ and fighting inner (and outer demons) demons. What´s the worst thing that could happen from having an 8 year old deformed teeth monster as a party member? Hugs.

The story is so good, the characters are very rich and enticing, the mystery behind The Visitor is fascinating, the OST is really good, reminds me of like spookier version of Undertale.
